pattern.title: Ended up: tuli tehtyy, tuli sanottuu
pattern.summary: 'Tuli' + a passive participle means you ended up doing something - often unplanned, often slightly regretted: tuli valvottuu = ended up staying up. No one is blamed; it just happened. Spoken Finnish stretches the ending: sanottua → sanottuu.
examples: Tuli taas valvottuu liian myöhään. = Ended up staying up too late again.
Tuli sanottuu vähän liikaa eilen. = I ended up saying a bit too much yesterday.
Ei tullu käytyy salilla koko viikolla. = Didn't end up going to the gym all week.

sentences.fi: Mul tuli just mieleen yks juttu.
sentences.written: Minulla tuli juuri mieleen yksi juttu.

glosses.mieleen: to mind - 'tulla mieleen' = to occur to you

sentences.en: Ended up staying up too late again.

glosses.tuli: it came to be - the ended-up verb

sentences.en: I ended up saying a bit too much yesterday.

glosses.tuli: it came to be - the ended-up verb

sentences.fi: Mulle tuli ostettuu ihan liikaa safkaa.
sentences.en: I ended up buying way too much food.
sentences.written: Minulle tuli ostettua aivan liikaa ruokaa.

glosses.tuli: it came to be - the ended-up verb

sentences.fi: Tuliks sul jo luettuu se viesti?
sentences.en: Did you end up reading that message yet?
sentences.written: Tuliko sinulla jo luettua se viesti?

glosses.tuliks: did it come to be - spoken 'tuliko'

sentences.fi: Ei tullu käytyy salilla koko viikolla.
sentences.en: Didn't end up going to the gym all week.
sentences.written: Ei tullut käytyä salilla koko viikolla.

sentences.en: I drank your coffee by accident.
Ended up being a long day.

glosses.tuli: it came to be - the ended-up verb
